meta.title: Sovereign Pluriversal Prompt Architecture
meta.short_tagline: An advanced epistemic framework for designing, stress-testing, and governing resilient LLM prompts across heterogeneous model ecosystems.

overview.title: Architectural Overview
overview.intro: The Sovereign Pluriversal Prompt Architecture represents a paradigm shift from linear prompt creation to recursive, multi-causal epistemic governance. It operates as an 'Epistemic Immune System' that hardens prompt patterns against model drift, hallucination, and behavioral degradation across diverse frontier models.

summary_card.label: Core Mechanism
summary_card.title: The Hickam-OODA Loop
summary_card.body: A non-linear processing loop that rejects parsimony (Occam's Razor) in favor of structural isomorphism and multi-causality (Hickam's Dictum), ensuring prompt systems are resilient to overlapping, contradictory failure modes.

key_concepts.title: Epistemic Pillars

cards.title: Epistemic Immune System
cards.body: An embedded architectural layer that actively detects and mitigates epistemic threats such as hallucination, sycophancy, instruction drift, and overfitting to a single model monoculture.

cards.title: Pluriversal Context Adaptation
cards.body: The deliberate design of prompts to operate effectively across heterogeneous model ecosystems (e.g., GPT, Gemini, Claude, and Open-Source), accommodating varying XML tolerances and system instruction behaviors.

cards.title: Symbolic Scars
cards.body: Documented drift risks, ambiguity zones, and epistemic tensions (e.g., Pedagogy vs. Throughput, Immune Overreach) that are tracked and mitigated via dynamic LENS configurations and prompt decorators.

cards.title: Pedagogical Constraint
cards.body: The mandate that every interaction must serve as a teaching moment, explicitly exposing design rationale, architectural tradeoffs, and failure-mode protections rather than silently optimizing.

key_concepts.intro: These core concepts form the foundation of a resilient, self-correcting prompt engineering methodology.

workflow.title: Operational Modes

steps.label: Mode 1: Build
steps.summary: Synthesize a production-ready prompt from a rough conceptual goal.
bullets: Extract required context via targeted clarifying questions.
Draft the prompt utilizing appropriate XML tags, system boundaries, and structural constraints.
Provide explicit design rationale explaining structural choices.
Offer iteration dimensions (e.g., strictness, creativity, output format).

steps.label: Mode 2 & 3: Analyze & Refactor
steps.summary: Audit and reconstruct existing prompts to eliminate structural vulnerabilities.
bullets: Perform structural and failure-mode audits on the target prompt.
Identify missing negative constraints and edge cases.
Extract core intent and resolve underlying contradictions.
Produce a cleaned, refactored version with a diff-style rationale summary.

steps.label: Mode 4: Architect
steps.summary: Design complex multi-component prompt architectures for advanced systems.
bullets: Map all necessary components including system prompts, tools, and memory parsers.
Design clear interfaces and define context-passing protocols.
Identify and map potential failure points such as context window limits.
Generate text-based architecture diagrams alongside component prompts.

steps.label: Mode 5: Stress-Test
steps.summary: Execute rigorous QA protocols to validate prompt resilience.
bullets: Generate test suites covering happy paths, edge cases, adversarial attacks, and contradictory inputs.
Predict likely failure modes for the prompt under test.
Suggest concrete modifications to harden the prompt against discovered vulnerabilities.

workflow.intro: The architecture operates across five primary modes to cover the entire lifecycle of prompt development and maintenance.

checklist.title: Prompt Deployment Checklist

items.label: Initial Scoping & Clarification
bullets: Define the target consumer (human, LLM, API).
Identify the target model family to determine structural tolerance.
Establish strict negative constraints (what the prompt MUST NOT do).

items.label: Structural Drafting
bullets: Utilize clear demarcations (e.g., XML tags like <instructions>, <context>).
Apply primacy and recency biases for the most critical instructions.
Separate system rules from user data flows.

items.label: Resilience Validation
bullets: Audit for hallucination and format drift vulnerabilities.
Generate adversarial inputs to ensure constraints hold.
Provide an explicit 'Design Rationale' document for future maintainers.

checklist.intro: Ensure these criteria are met before migrating a prompt from drafting to production environments.

metrics.intro: Quantitative and qualitative metrics utilized to evaluate the epistemic health of prompt architectures.

items.name: Martensite Initiation Quotient (MIQ)
items.description: Calculates the structural tension and intent divergence of a generated solution.
signals: Intent Divergence Risk (Legitimacy collapse vs. Social Consensus)
Twinning Strategy Application (Nuance integration for high tension)

items.name: Contrastive Delta
items.description: Measures the difference between a generic, single-cause response and a high-tension, multi-causal expert correction.

items.name: Severity Rating Audit
items.description: Classifies structural flaws and failure mode vulnerabilities within a prompt.

metrics.title: Evaluation Metrics

personas.intro: The dual-perspective framework utilized to govern prompt engineering interactions.

cells: Structural topology, component mapping, logic flow
Multi-agent architectures, structured tags, context limits
Model interpretation, attention mechanics, failure modes
Constraint tuning, sycophancy mitigation, instruction primacy

personas.title: Architectural Personas

failure_modes.name: Model Monoculture Drift (SCAR-1)
failure_modes.description: Over-tuning prompt structures to GPT-style behaviors, causing failures when deployed to Claude, Gemini, or Open-Source models.
mitigations: Implement explicit LENS decorators for specific model families.
Avoid assuming a universal baseline for XML tolerance and system-prompt strictness.

failure_modes.name: Memory Staleness (SCAR-10)
failure_modes.description: Persistent memory systems fossilizing outdated practices or conflicting user preferences over time.
mitigations: Enforce periodic memory hygiene and 'sunset' markers on older patterns.
Require explicit confirmation before applying preferences older than 5 interaction cycles.

failure_modes.name: Immune Overreach (SCAR-3)
failure_modes.description: Strict negative constraints (e.g., 'Never generate final content') blocking necessary user debugging or intuitive demonstrations.
mitigations: Enable a 'sandboxed' content demo mode that produces clearly labeled, partial, non-production examples.

resilience.intro: Known vulnerabilities (Symbolic Scars) within prompt engineering systems and their structural mitigations.
resilience.title: System Resilience & Symbolic Scars

structure.intro: The 15-Layer Prompt Stack categorizing the depth and function of LLM instructions.

cells: Defines core persona, pluriversal alignment, and foundational values.
Establishes operational boundaries, failure mode defenses, and exact responsibilities.
Dictates workflow modes, XML/structural patterns, and multi-model specific tuning.
Governs persistent memory, tool interfaces, stress-testing, and self-correction loops.

structure.title: The 15-Layer Context-Intent-Ethics Stack
